articles.title: Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
articles.summary: Iran's IRGC published a video threatening OpenAI's $30 billion Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi, warning of retaliation if the US attacks Iranian power plants.

articles.title: Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins wants data centers in space
articles.summary: Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins discusses the company's vision for establishing data centers in space to support future internet infrastructure and connectivity needs.

articles.title: Suno is a music copyright nightmare
articles.summary: Suno's AI music platform claims to prevent copyrighted material use, but its copyright filters are easily bypassed. Users can generate AI imitations of popular songs with minimal effort using free software.

articles.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
articles.summary: Google launched an offline AI dictation app powered by Gemma models, competing with apps like Whisper Flow. The app functions without internet connection.

articles.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
articles.summary: Iran threatens to strike U.S.-linked data centers with missile attacks, escalating tensions between the two nations amid ongoing conflict.

articles.title: OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day work week
articles.summary: OpenAI proposes AI profit taxes, public wealth funds, and expanded safety nets to address job displacement and inequality, blending redistribution with capitalism amid economic concerns.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud by addressing limitations in legacy platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code costs $20-$200 monthly for AI coding assistance, but Goose offers identical functionality as a free, open-source alternative running locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The unconventional marketing stunt helped recruit engineers in a competitive talent market.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agentic tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks by requiring broad system access. A recently patched vulnerability highlights dangers of granting such extensive permissions to AI agents.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: New Rowhammer attacks on Nvidia GPUs enable malicious users to gain full root control of shared cloud machines by exploiting memory bit flips through repeated memory access.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: Recent research shows quantum computers could break elliptic curve encryption using vastly fewer resources than previously thought. Two papers demonstrate breaking 256-bit ECC in 10 days and cryptocurrency blockchain encryption in under 9 minutes.

articles.title: The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI
articles.summary: Silicon Valley leaders predict AI will cause job displacement, with some claiming AI could replace all human jobs within five years. Researchers warn of potential recessions and breakdown of early-career opportunities.

articles.title: AI is changing how small online sellers decide what to make
articles.summary: AI tools like Alibaba's Accio are transforming how small online sellers find manufacturers and decide what products to make, replacing traditional time-consuming supplier research with automated sourcing.

articles.title: Four things we’d need to put data centers in space
articles.summary: SpaceX proposes launching orbital data centers to support AI growth without environmental impact on Earth. Tech giants like Amazon and Google are exploring space-based computing infrastructure.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, a new frontier multimodal AI model designed to run efficiently on devices. It combines advanced intelligence with on-device capabilities for improved privacy and performance.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer use AI, advancing the frontier of how artificial intelligence interacts with digital systems and interfaces.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: Falcon Perception represents advances in AI visual processing and object detection capabilities. The system demonstrates improved accuracy in identifying and analyzing visual information from images and video.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its potential impact. The conversation explores upcoming developments and innovations in the artificial intelligence field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing. These AI solutions help companies streamline workflows and generate more imaginative campaigns.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit to advance agricultural technology and sustainability. The toolkit aims to help farmers leverage AI and digital tools for improved productivity.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces Flex and Priority inference tiers for the Gemini API to balance cost efficiency with latency performance, offering developers flexible options.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling users to generate high-quality videos at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map aimed at protecting Brazil's forests from deforestation and environmental threats.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source AI models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, delivering improved intelligence across various tasks.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live improves voice AI with enhanced precision and lower latency, enabling more natural and fluid voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect people from harmful outcomes.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and higher per-token prices but improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family featuring mixture-of-experts architecture.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics tool that enhances video game photorealism in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate realistic details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, and new interactive features from ChatGPT and Anthropic for visualizations.

articles.title: As AI agents take on more tasks, governance becomes a priority
articles.summary: AI agents are evolving beyond simple responses to autonomously plan, decide, and act, requiring robust governance frameworks with clear boundaries, access controls, and action tracking to prevent unintended consequences.

articles.title: KiloClaw targets shadow AI with autonomous agent governance
articles.summary: KiloClaw is an enterprise platform that governs autonomous agents and manages shadow AI deployments. It addresses the 'Bring Your Own AI' trend where employees bypass procurement to deploy agents on personal infrastructure.

articles.title: 5 best practices to secure AI systems
articles.summary: AI systems require multi-layered security strategies including data protection, access control, and monitoring. Five foundational practices address emerging risks from embedded AI in critical operations.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agents cause task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, advancing collaborative AI problem-solving.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance developed Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them are emerging as the real competitive advantage. The Anthropic Claude Code leak demonstrates that infrastructure and integration engineering, not just base models, determine market differentiation.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, addressing a major challenge in AI model development.

articles.title: RightNow AI Releases AutoKernel: An Open-Source Framework that Applies an Autonomous Agent Loop to GPU Kernel Optimization for Arbitrary PyTorch Models
articles.summary: RightNow AI released AutoKernel, an open-source framework using autonomous LLM agents to automatically optimize GPU kernels for PyTorch models, eliminating the need for GPU expertise.

articles.title: Meet MaxToki: The AI That Predicts How Your Cells Age — and What to Do About It
articles.summary: MaxToki is an AI model that predicts cellular aging trajectories over time, overcoming the limitation of traditional models that only analyze static cell snapshots. It enables understanding of age-related diseases by tracking progressive gene network changes.

articles.title: How to Build a Netflix VOID Video Object Removal and Inpainting Pipeline with CogVideoX, Custom Prompting, and End-to-End Sample Inference
articles.summary: Tutorial on building Netflix's VOID model pipeline for video object removal and inpainting using CogVideoX. Covers environment setup, model configuration, inference execution, and result visualization.

articles.title: The best Android phones of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
articles.summary: Expert review of the best Android phones of 2026, featuring top devices from Samsung and Google that stand out from the competition.

articles.title: I tested Gemini on Android Auto and now I can't stop talking to it: 5 tasks it nails
articles.summary: Google's Gemini AI integration on Android Auto proved surprisingly useful for car tasks. The AI assistant excels at various driving-related functions, making it a valuable addition for mobile users.

articles.title: I used a single power station to keep my off-grid cabin running - how it all worked out
articles.summary: The author tested multiple power stations and selected the Bluetti Apex 300 as the best option for powering their off-grid cabin.

articles.title: Top 5 Reranking Models to Improve RAG Results
articles.summary: This article discusses the top 5 reranking models that enhance retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system performance by improving result relevance and accuracy.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are evolving beyond backend dashboards into more visible applications. Key trends to watch in 2026 include advancing capabilities and broader deployment across industries.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: The article discusses state-managed interruptions in agentic AI systems, where human oversight halts agent execution pipelines. This creates approval gates enabling human-in-the-loop control over autonomous agent operations.

articles.title: The Geometry Behind the Dot Product: Unit Vectors, Projections, and Intuition
articles.summary: Article explores the geometric foundations of dot products, covering unit vectors, projections, and intuitive understanding of this fundamental mathematical concept.

articles.title: How to Run Claude Code Agents in Parallel
articles.summary: Learn techniques to run Claude code agents in parallel for improved efficiency and faster task execution.

articles.title: Behavior is the New Credential
articles.summary: Authentication is shifting from knowledge-based and biometric methods to behavioral analysis. Online identity verification now focuses on how users act rather than what they know or look like.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: NVIDIA highlights physical AI breakthroughs during National Robotics Week, showcasing robots transforming industries. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment from virtual training.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution on various devices, from RTX PCs to data centers, enabling efficient on-device agentic AI with real-time context processing.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches ten new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield. The cloud gaming service expands its library with fresh titles for subscribers.

articles.title: Build AI-powered employee onboarding agents with Amazon Quick
articles.summary: Amazon Quick enables HR teams to create AI-powered onboarding agents that automate employee onboarding tasks, answer questions about benefits and policies, and track compliance without coding, reducing manual work and accelerating new hire productivity.

articles.title: Accelerate agentic tool calling with serverless model customization in Amazon SageMaker AI
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker AI's serverless model customization uses RLVR to improve agentic tool calling, reducing hallucinations and parameter errors without managing infrastructure.

articles.title: Building Intelligent Search with Amazon Bedrock and Amazon OpenSearch for hybrid RAG solutions
articles.summary: Agentic AI assistants powered by LLMs combine intelligent dialogue with real-time data retrieval through APIs and databases, enabling Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for dynamic, context-aware responses in applications like hotel booking.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA accelerates vision AI pipelines using batch mode VC-6 codec and Nsight tools to improve throughput across decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Gemma 4 launches with multimodal and multilingual capabilities, scaling from data centers to edge devices via NVIDIA Blackwell and Jetson platforms.

articles.title: Achieving Single-Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets
articles.summary: NVIDIA enables single-digit microsecond latency AI inference for algorithmic trading, allowing firms to compete with high-speed electronic markets using advanced neural network models instead of traditional FPGAs.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, exploring generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology shared openly. The company focuses on reducing its environmental footprint through innovation.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background developed cutting-edge extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive innovation in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers develop methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and understand model decision-making processes for safer, more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents an information-driven approach to design imaging systems by using noisy measurements and noise models to quantify how well measurements distinguish objects. The method optimizes optical systems for better image quality.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm using divide-and-conquer paradigm is introduced as an alternative to temporal difference learning, addressing scalability challenges for long-horizon tasks.
